making_charges_high.label: Making charges are high
recommended_responses: Acknowledge calmly first and avoid sounding defensive.
Ask what the customer is comparing the making charges with: earlier purchase, another jeweller, or online.
Explain that jewellery price differences come from design, craftsmanship, process, finishing, comfort, and accountability, not only weight.
Explain that making charges include gold consumed or lost during melting, casting, cutting, filing, stone setting, polishing, hallmarking, certification, and quality control.
Explain that wastage is charged as a percentage, so the same wastage becomes costlier when the gold price rises.
Reframe the piece as handcrafted art, longevity, comfort, and pride before discussing any adjustment.
If offering relief, use language like special expert consideration or adjustment, not discount positioning.
forbidden_responses: Do not defend immediately or argue with the customer.
Do not start with discount, best rate, last price, or special price language.
Do not say making is only labour charge.
Do not imply the karigar randomly increased charges when the wastage percentage is unchanged.
Do not skip the value explanation and jump straight to price reduction.

price_value_concern.label: General price or value concern
recommended_responses: Before talking about price, ask what matters most to the customer in the piece.
Build value through beauty, trust, emotion, expertise, experience, and future security.
Use styling and emotional lines: explain how the piece suits the customer, occasion, lifestyle, or family memory.
Use proof over promise: certificates, store legacy, BIS HUID, certified diamonds, buyback, exchange, quality checks, and long-term support.
Stay silent after quoting the price instead of over-explaining.
forbidden_responses: Do not begin the conversation with gold rate, making charges, or discount.
Do not say many people buy this as generic social proof.
Do not ask bluntly whether the customer wants to buy.
Do not chase, argue, or push.

diamond_buyback_deduction.label: Diamond buyback deduction or valuation concern
recommended_responses: Welcome the customer, receive jewellery carefully, and explain that every component is calculated separately.
Verify original invoice and authenticity certificate; if unavailable, explain that valuation can still happen but documentation is required before payment.
Explain gross weight, diamond weight, non-gold components, and net gold weight before discussing policy.
Explain diamond quality and current market value before explaining the 10% diamond buyback adjustment.
Say the gold portion is calculated separately based on today's live gold rate and net gold weight.
If the customer compares purchase and buyback value, first compare diamond quality, then explain that buying and buyback are different transactions, then explain the policy.
Use current market value, net gold weight, transparent calculation, every component is valued separately, fair valuation, and today's live gold rate.
forbidden_responses: Do not start by discussing deductions.
Do not say we deduct 10% or we cut 10%.
Do not say loss, depreciation, your jewellery has become cheaper, or we only give this much.
Do not guess the valuation or commit to an amount before technical assessment.
Do not compare with competitors, debate, or say this is our rate, take it or leave it.

diamond_valuation_without_invoice.label: Diamond valuation without invoice or immediate valuation
customer_signals: don't want to leave the jewellery
recommended_responses: Say the invoice helps identify specifications faster, but evaluation can still be done without it.
Explain that diamond jewellery valuation verifies gold purity, net gold weight, diamond weight, diamond quality, craftsmanship, and current market value.
Set an expectation that complete evaluation generally takes 24-48 hours because accuracy is preferred over a quick estimate.
If the customer needs an answer today, offer only a broad indicative range after visual inspection.
Confirm final valuation only after complete technical assessment.
forbidden_responses: Do not begin by asking do you have the bill.
Do not say factory bhejna padega or factory will decide.
Do not say it should be around a specific amount before assessment.
Do not guess, argue, or commit to a final value immediately.

old_gold_exchange.label: Old gold sale or exchange
recommended_responses: Explain that MK Jewels purchases old gold at the same standard gold buying rate used for bullion-market purchase.
Explain that old gold becomes raw material and is refined and reused, so scientific purity testing is required.
Inspect and remove meenakari, stones, pearls, enamel, adhesives, and other non-gold elements before weighing.
Explain that final calculation is based on post-melting weight and karatometer purity.
Take written consent before melting and explain the process is irreversible and final purity may vary.
Explain that 1% is deducted as a standard refining charge, not profit or margin.
Offer credit note, exchange against new jewellery, or bank transfer where applicable, excluding coins or bullion.
forbidden_responses: Do not commit any estimate as final without melting.
Do not accept gold with non-removable stones unless clearly agreed by the customer.
Do not hide that non-gold elements are excluded from gold-rate valuation.
Do not skip melting consent or final transaction signatures.

certification_authenticity.label: Certification and authenticity question
recommended_responses: Explain that MK Jewels jewellery is BIS HUID hallmarked and diamonds are certified.
For IGI certified natural diamond bangles, explain that the certificate can be scanned through its QR code and verified online.
Explain that the certificate mentions gold weight and exact diamond specifications including colour and clarity.
Explain that the same certificate number is engraved on the bangle as proof of authenticity.
Position authenticity as documentation, transparency, and luxury together.
forbidden_responses: Do not ask the customer to trust blindly when verification is available.
Do not make unsupported certification claims beyond the certificate.
Do not ignore certificate, QR code, or engraved certificate-number questions.

occasion_styling_discovery.label: Missed occasion, styling, or discovery opportunity
recommended_responses: Ask who the jewellery is for and whether it is for a special occasion or everyday elegance.
Guide like a consultant, not a stock-shower.
Discuss face shape, neckline, outfit, occasion, and lifestyle.
Use soft, honest compliments and mirror-moment lines when the customer tries the jewellery.
Translate features into feelings: confidence, elegance, celebration, status, memories, and peace of mind.
forbidden_responses: Do not only show stock without discovery.
Do not use loud, fake, or overexcited compliments.
Do not reduce an emotional occasion purchase to only gold rate, making charge, or discount.
